authoritative_sources.title: Comptroller's Handbook: Payment Systems
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official OCC supervisory handbook content for banks that defines payment workflow stages, payment types, and risk-management expectations for ACH, wire, and wholesale payment activity relevant to commercial banking operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Execution-state design, payment-stage naming, and completion or reconciliation evidence
used_for: lifecycle stage definitions from initiation through reconciliation
control points for authentication, authorization, approval, clearing, settlement, and reconciliation

authoritative_sources.title: Authentication and Access to Financial Institution Services and Systems
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official interagency FFIEC guidance for financial institutions that sets access and authentication risk-management expectations for customers, employees, and third parties using digital banking services and systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Signer, entitlement, callback, and layered-control validation before commercial service fulfillment
used_for: boundary against weak or single-factor control handling

authoritative_sources.title: Operating Circulars
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Public Federal Reserve Financial Services operating rules govern Reserve Bank financial services, including Operating Circular 4 for commercial ACH items and Operating Circular 6 for Fedwire funds transfers used in commercial banking workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Commercial ACH and Fedwire setup, cutoff-aware handling, and rail-specific workflow boundaries
used_for: commercial ACH and Fedwire scope boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: FFIEC BSA/AML Manual - Funds Transfers Recordkeeping
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official interagency examination-manual section used by U.S. banking regulators to assess funds-transfer recordkeeping, Travel Rule obligations, and required supporting records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Record-of-truth establishment for wire and funds-transfer evidence, exception handling, and audit trail preservation

authoritative_sources.title: FFIEC BSA/AML Manual - Suspicious Activity Reporting
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official interagency suspicious-activity-reporting guidance that defines escalation posture, confidentiality expectations, and examination standards for suspicious activity in banking operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Fraud or AML exception handling and refusal to disclose investigative posture

authoritative_sources.title: FFIEC BSA/AML Manual - Customer Due Diligence
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FFIEC exam procedures that direct banks to collect information sufficient to understand the nature and purpose of customer relationships and to incorporate beneficial-ownership information for legal-entity customers into the customer risk profile.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Commercial legal-entity authority, signer, and customer-risk prerequisite validation
used_for: blocked-state triggers when documentary authority is incomplete

authoritative_sources.title: Information on Complying with the Customer Due Diligence (CDD) Final Rule
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FinCEN source for current CDD rule posture, including the February 13, 2026 alert and exceptive relief that changes how institutions should interpret beneficial-owner identification and verification at each account opening.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Current-state source refresh and conflict handling for legal-entity account or service-opening prerequisites
used_for: non-blocking ambiguity recording on beneficial-owner refresh posture

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 20022 Financial Services Messaging Framework
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized international financial-message standard that provides common financial-services terminology and message-repository structure used to normalize payment and reconciliation artifacts across systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cross-system artifact naming, reconciliation, and evidence normalization

workflow_stages.name: Commercial request intake and legal-entity grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the legal entity, account, service, jurisdiction, customer relationship, and request type before acting, and determine whether the work is account maintenance, signer or entitlement servicing, payment setup, or owner-transfer sensitive.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Authority, documentary, and access-control validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify documentary authority such as board resolutions, secretary certificates, signer matrices, beneficial-owner or CDD posture, approval thresholds, callback rules, and layered-access controls before changing commercial services or payment access.

workflow_stages.name: Execution readiness and rail-aware fulfillment
workflow_stages.description: Use accepted workflow state, payment-rail context, cutoff posture, and service prerequisites to determine whether a request can advance into fulfillment, stay blocked, or move to rework.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Exception, rework, and completion-evidence handling
workflow_stages.description: Compare core-banking, treasury portal, payment-hub, document, and communication records before declaring completion, and keep work in hold or rework status when completion evidence is incomplete or contradictory.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Fraud, AML, and customer-impact escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when unusual payment behavior, documentary mismatch, or suspicious-activity indicators materially change the answer and avoid disclosing SAR posture or making investigative conclusions.

workflow_stages.name: Source refresh, policy conflict, and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Stop short of confident synthesis when current FinCEN or FFIEC posture, tenant policy, product mix, or payment-rail usage changes the safe handling path, and document the unresolved conflict explicitly.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

artifacts: legal-entity customer profile, CIF, account records, relationship metadata, and service request identifiers
board resolutions, secretary certificates, signature cards, signer matrices, beneficial-owner or CDD artifacts, and tax forms
treasury-management enrollments, entitlement logs, ACH company IDs, wire templates, callback records, cutoff schedules, and approval history
core-banking records, treasury-portal records, payment-hub records, servicing notes, case-management notes, and customer communication logs
fraud-monitoring, AML review, KYC or CIP, exception-queue, and escalation artifacts
general-ledger, reconciliation, and message-normalization artifacts used to confirm commercial-payment or service truth
systems_of_record: fraud monitoring and AML review systems

allowed: Classify commercial banking operations requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked based on documented request type, authority evidence, and workflow state.
Request the minimum missing legal-entity, account, service, approval, or system context needed to proceed safely.
Prepare execution updates, completion records, exception summaries, and escalation packets grounded in systems-of-record evidence.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work only when the documentary, payment, access, and communication records support the documented lifecycle state.
escalate: Required tenant facts, authority records, or current-source context are missing or contradictory.
The request seeks an unsupported override of documentary controls, dual control, callback, cutoff, approval thresholds, or suspicious-activity boundaries.
Fraud, AML, customer-impact, treasury settlement, or legal-interpretation signals appear.
Public guidance, current FinCEN updates, and tenant policy materially disagree on the safe workflow.
refuse: Requests for legal interpretation of authority documents, account agreements, or lending documents.
Requests to approve credit, underwriting, treasury funding, collateral release, or other authority-side decisions.
Requests to fabricate approvals, treat incomplete records as final truth, or disclose SAR or investigative posture.

domain_failure_modes: activating signer, ACH, or wire access without authoritative legal-entity documentary support
treating CRM or a local queue note as final completion evidence when core, treasury, payment, or communication records conflict
bypassing dual-control, callback, or cutoff handling to satisfy an urgent client request
using generic retail-banking or customer-support language instead of commercial-banking artifact and authority terminology
disclosing or implying suspicious-activity or SAR posture during ordinary client servicing
failing to separate deposit-service execution from treasury-settlement, credit, underwriting, or legal authority
guessing on beneficial-owner or legal-entity prerequisite posture after current-source updates or tenant-policy conflict

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Boundary rejection for adjacent functional specialists
Tenant-specific adaptation with multi-entity control differences

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Beneficial-owner refresh posture after February 13, 2026 FinCEN exceptive relief
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Commercial banks may treat new sub-accounts, service boardings, or maintenance events differently from new account openings, and tenant policy may remain stricter than the updated public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling_rule: Require tenant policy and compliance-owner precedence before applying beneficial-owner refresh logic, and block confident fulfillment if the difference would change the outcome.
